title: Leiotulus isfahanicus
intro: Leiotulus isfahanicus is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to the Middle East and Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as respiratory issues and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Leiotulus isfahanicus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Leiotulus isfahanicus is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to relieve symptoms of coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Leiotulus isfahanicus is classified as a member of the family Boraginaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. It belongs to the genus Leiotulus, which is a small genus of flowering plants. Within the genus Leiotulus, Leiotulus isfahanicus is further classified as a distinct species. The plant's classification also places it within the order Lamiales, which includes many other flowering plant families. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics, which are used to identify and group plant species.
